Home 01 LLMs 02 Prompts 03 API 04 Tools 05 Embeddings 06 RAG 07 Agents 08 Structured 09 Streaming 10 Evals 11 Ethics 12 Capstone Use Cases Games Projects

Portfolio Projects

Four production AI applications built for journalism. Each demonstrates practical LLM integration, prompt engineering, and responsible AI patterns for newsroom workflows.

Claude API RAG Pipeline Tool Use Journalism
Project 01

AI Newsroom Assistant

A conversational AI assistant for newsroom staff that drafts story briefs, researches background context, generates interview questions, and summarizes documents. Built with Claude API, tool use, and conversation memory.

Claude Messages API tool use conversation memory streaming system prompts
Reporter
I need to write a brief on the new city housing ordinance passed yesterday. Pull the key details.
AI Assistant
search_documents("city housing ordinance 2024")
AI Assistant
Here's your story brief:

HEADLINE: City Council Passes Inclusionary Housing Ordinance

KEY FACTS:
• Requires 15% affordable units in new developments over 10 units
• Passed 7-2 along party lines (Council members Rivera and Chen dissenting)
• Effective January 1, 2025
• Developers receive density bonus in exchange

CONTEXT: This follows 18 months of public hearings. Similar ordinances in Portland and Austin saw mixed results.

Sources: City Council minutes (3/15), Planning Commission report, HUD data

How I Built This

  • Built on the Claude Messages API with system prompt defining the newsroom assistant persona, AP style rules, and ethical guidelines
  • Tool use with search_documents, get_public_record, and generate_questions tools for research workflow
  • Conversation memory via message history array, with automatic summarization when context exceeds 150K tokens
  • Streaming responses via stream: true for real-time typewriter UX, critical for long research responses
  • Story brief template uses structured prompting: headline, key facts, context, sources, and suggested follow-ups
  • Safety layer checks AI outputs for unverified claims and flags them with [VERIFY] markers for the reporter
Project 02

Source Intelligence Platform

A RAG-powered research tool that ingests public records, court filings, campaign finance data, and government documents, then lets reporters ask natural-language questions across the entire corpus.

RAG pipeline embeddings vector search text-to-SQL hybrid retrieval
Document Ingestion
PDFs, court filings, campaign records → chunked, embedded, stored in vector DB
Structured Data
Campaign finance, property records, business filings → PostgreSQL tables
↓ query routing ↓
Hybrid Retrieval Engine
Vector search (semantic) + BM25 (keyword) + SQL (structured) → Reciprocal Rank Fusion → Re-ranking with cross-encoder
↓ context assembly ↓
Claude Generation
Grounded answer with source citations, confidence scores, and [UNVERIFIED] markers for claims beyond retrieved evidence

How I Built This

  • Documents chunked at 512 tokens with 100-token overlap using recursive character splitting for optimal retrieval granularity
  • Embeddings generated via voyage-3 model (1024 dimensions), stored in PostgreSQL with pgvector extension
  • Hybrid retrieval combines vector similarity, BM25 keyword scoring, and SQL queries using Reciprocal Rank Fusion
  • Cross-encoder re-ranker scores the top 20 candidates down to 5, dramatically improving precision
  • Query routing classifies questions as: structured (SQL), unstructured (vector search), or hybrid, then dispatches accordingly
  • All answers include inline citations: [Source: Filing #12345, p.7] so reporters can verify every claim
Project 03

Automated Fact-Checker

An agent-based system that extracts verifiable claims from text, searches for evidence across multiple sources, and produces structured verdicts with confidence levels. Designed for pre-publication review workflows.

agent loop multi-tool claim extraction evidence scoring human-in-the-loop
1
Claim Extraction
LLM identifies verifiable factual claims from article draft, ignoring opinions and qualifications
↓
2
Evidence Search (Agent Loop)
For each claim: search web, query databases, check previous fact-checks. Agent iterates until confident or exhausts sources.
↓
3
Verdict Generation
Rates each claim: True / Mostly True / Misleading / False / Unverifiable. Includes evidence summary and sources.
↓
4
Editor Review Dashboard
Human editor reviews AI verdicts, approves or overrides, and marks article for publication

How I Built This

  • Claim extraction uses a specialized prompt that identifies only verifiable factual statements, filtering out opinions, hedges, and attributions
  • Agent loop implements a while loop with tool calls: web_search, database_query, check_snopes, search_archive
  • Each evidence piece receives a relevance score (0-1) and a stance classification (supports / contradicts / neutral)
  • Verdict generation synthesizes all evidence into a structured rating with the 5-point PolitiFact-style scale
  • Human-in-the-loop dashboard shows claims, evidence, AI verdict, and lets the editor accept, modify, or reject each finding
  • Evaluation suite measures precision/recall against a hand-labeled dataset of 500 claims from past publications
Project 04

AI-Powered FOIA Tool

End-to-end FOIA workflow: generates legally compliant requests, tracks submissions, processes returned documents, and extracts newsworthy findings. Built for investigative reporters who file dozens of records requests.

document generation tracking system PDF processing anomaly detection vision model
Request Generator
Reporter describes records needed → AI generates FOIA letter with correct agency, statutes, fee waiver, and legal citations
Tracker Dashboard
Status tracking: Submitted → Acknowledged → Processing → Partial Release → Complete. Auto-generates appeal letters for denials.
↓ documents received ↓
Document Processor
Vision model handles scanned PDFs. OCR + extraction pipeline outputs structured data: names, dates, amounts, communications.
Finding Analyzer
Cross-references extracted data against public records. Flags anomalies: unusual spending, conflicts of interest, timeline gaps.
↓
Story Lead Report
Generates a prioritized list of newsworthy findings with evidence links, suggested story angles, and additional records to request.
Requests Filed
47
Across 12 agencies
Docs Processed
2,341
Pages analyzed
Leads Found
8
Story-worthy findings
Time Saved
~120h
vs manual review

How I Built This

  • Request generator uses RAG over a database of successful FOIA templates, state-specific statutes, and agency contact information
  • Fee waiver section cites the reporter's press credentials and public interest test, customized per jurisdiction's legal requirements
  • Document processor uses Claude's vision capabilities to handle scanned, redacted, and handwritten government documents
  • Extraction pipeline outputs structured JSON: {entity, date, amount, document_ref, page_num, confidence} for every extracted fact
  • Anomaly detection cross-references extracted data against campaign finance, property, and business registration databases
  • The story lead report ranks findings by newsworthiness using criteria: public impact, dollar amount, number of affected people, and novelty
